products.title: Fiber Cable 12 Core Multi Mode OM2 50/125 GYXTW
products.subtitle: Outdoor Fiber Optic Cable, 12 Core, Steel Armored, PE, Jelly filled, GYXTW, Multi Mode OM2 50/125 9.0mm.
products.description: The Netsys NS-402OM2012 is a high-quality 12-core OM2 Multimode fiber optic cable designed for high-capacity backbone installations and internal network distribution. Featuring a standard 50/125µm core/cladding diameter, this cable is the reliable choice for Gigabit Ethernet (1GbE) applications, providing a stable and cost-efficient fiber solution for enterprise environments. Technical Specifications: Feature Details Brand/Model Netsys NS-402OM2012 Fiber Category OM2 Multimode Fiber Count 12 Cores Core/Cladding 50/125µm 1G Ethernet Support Up to 600 Meters (@ 850nm) 10G Ethernet Support Up to 82 Meters (@ 850nm) Bandwidth (OFL) 500 MHz·km @ 850nm / 500 MHz·km @ 1300nm Standard Jacket Color Orange Key Product Features 12-Core Connectivity: Provides a balanced density for standard network cabinets, allowing for multiple duplex links or dedicated paths for different network segments. Enhanced 50/125µm Fiber: Offers lower attenuation and higher bandwidth than older 62.5µm (OM1) cables, allowing for longer distance runs at Gigabit speeds. Streamlined Termination: The 12-fiber internal structure follows standard color-coding protocols, making it compatible with all standard LC, SC, and ST connectors and splice trays. Versatile Application: Primarily optimized for LED-based transmission equipment while maintaining compatibility with legacy laser sources. Common Applications Building Risers: Vertical cabling connecting different floors of an office building or commercial complex. LAN Backbones: Establishing high-speed links between main distribution frames (MDF) and intermediate distribution frames (IDF). Industrial Controls: Reliable data transmission for manufacturing environments and automated systems. CCTV & Security: Handling high-definition video data over distances that exceed the limits of traditional copper Ethernet.

products.title: Fiber Cable 12 Core Multi Mode OM3 50/125 GYXTW
products.subtitle: Outdoor Fiber Optic Cable, 12 Core, Steel Armored, PE, Jelly filled, GYXTW, Multi Mode OM3 50/125 9.0mm.
products.description: The NS-403OM3012 is a professional-grade, 12-core OM3 Multimode fiber optic cable, engineered to provide high-speed data transmission for mid-to-high density networking environments. As a Laser-Optimized Multimode Fiber (LOMMF), it is specifically designed for use with 850nm VCSELs, making it the industry standard for reliable 10G Ethernet backbones. Technical Specifications: NS-403OM3012 Feature Details Model Number NS-403OM3012 Fiber Category OM3 Multimode (Laser Optimized) Fiber Count 12 Cores Core/Cladding Diameter 50/125µm Max 10G Distance 300 Meters Max 40G/100G Distance 100 Meters Bandwidth (EMB) 2000 MHz·km @ 850nm Attenuation $\leq 3.0$ dB/km @ 850nm Key Performance Features Optimized for 10GbE: Delivers seamless 10 Gigabit performance up to 300 meters, providing a perfect balance between cost and high-speed capability. Standard 12-Core Configuration: The 12-fiber count is ideal for MPO/MTP termination and standard patch panel deployments, offering a clean, organized trunking solution. Small Form Factor: The 50/125µm construction allows for higher port density and easier routing through narrow conduits compared to older OM1/OM2 standards. Backward Compatibility: Fully compatible with existing OM4 infrastructure, allowing for easy integration into hybrid multimode networks. Ideal Use Cases Vertical/Horizontal Backbones: Connecting telecommunications closets to main distribution frames. Server Room Links: High-speed interconnects between switches, routers, and storage devices. Enterprise LANs: Providing high-bandwidth support for video conferencing, VoIP, and cloud-based applications.

products.title: Fiber Cable 12 Core Multi Mode OM4 50/125 GYXTW
products.subtitle: Outdoor Fiber Optic Cable, 12 Core, Steel Armored, PE, Jelly filled, GYXTW, Multi Mode OM4 50/125 9.0mm.
products.description: The Netsys NS-404OM4012 is a high-performance, 12-core OM4 multi-mode fiber optic cable designed to meet the demands of modern, high-bandwidth data environments. Whether you’re scaling a data center or backbone infrastructure, this cable is built for 40G and 100G Ethernet applications. Key Specifications Feature Detail Model Number NS-404OM4012 Fiber Count 12 Cores Fiber Type Multi-Mode OM4 Core/Cladding Diameter 50/125µm Bandwidth (EMB) 4700 MHz·km @ 850nm Jacket Material LSZH (Low Smoke Zero Halogen) or PVC (Depending on variant) Core Benefits Future-Proof Performance: Optimized for laser-based equipment (VCSEL), the OM4 fiber supports 10Gbps up to 550 meters and 40/100Gbps up to 150 meters, providing a significant edge over standard OM3 cables. High Density: With 12 individual fiber cores bundled into a single jacket, it maximizes space in conduits and patch panels, making it ideal for high-density networking racks. Low Signal Loss: The 50/125 micron construction ensures minimal attenuation and high-speed data integrity over medium distances. Versatile Application: Excellent for backbone cabling, horizontal distribution, and interconnecting high-speed switches in enterprise-level LANs. Technical Overview The NS-404OM4012 is "Laser Optimized," meaning it is specifically designed to eliminate Differential Mode Delay (DMD), which can occur when using high-speed laser light sources. This ensures that your data arrives intact and at the highest possible speeds.

products.title: Fiber Cable 12 Core Single Mode OS2 9/125 GYXTW
products.subtitle: Fiber cable, 12core, Outdoor, Armored, PE, Jelly filled, GYXTW, SM 9/125 (G652D) 9.0mm words engrave on the cable-blue color line
products.description: The Netsys NS-401SM012 is a professional-grade 12-core OS2 Singlemode fiber optic cable, engineered for high-speed, long-distance data transmission. Featuring a precision 9/125µm core/cladding diameter, this carrier-class cable is the industry standard for backbone infrastructure, providing virtually unlimited bandwidth potential and minimal signal attenuation over several kilometers. Technical Specifications: Feature Details Brand/Model Netsys NS-401SM012 Fiber Category OS2 Singlemode (Low Water Peak) Fiber Count 12 Cores Core/Cladding 9/125µm Transmission Distance 10km to 40km+ (Transceiver dependent) Data Rate Support 1G, 10G, 40G, 100G, 400G Ethernet Attenuation $\leq 0.35$ dB/km @ 1310nm; $\leq 0.22$ dB/km @ 1550nm Standard Jacket Color Yellow (Standard for OS2) Key Product Features High-Performance OS2 Glass: Optimized with "Low Water Peak" characteristics to ensure peak performance across the entire 1260nm to 1625nm wavelength spectrum. 12-Core Standard Configuration: Perfectly sized for standard 12-port fiber patch panels and MPO/MTP termination, offering a clean and scalable solution for network closets. Future-Proof Bandwidth: Unlike multimode fibers, OS2 Singlemode has no modal dispersion limits, making it fully compatible with next-generation 400G and 800G hardware. TIA/EIA Color-Coded Cores: All 12 fibers are individually color-coded for rapid identification, reducing installation time and errors during fusion splicing. Robust Environmental Shielding: Features a durable outer jacket and internal strength members (typically aramid yarn) to protect the glass cores during high-tension pulls. Primary Applications Building-to-Building Links: Creating high-speed, interference-free connections across a corporate or university campus. Enterprise Backbones: Serving as the primary vertical riser or horizontal link for large-scale networking environments. ISP & Telecom Infrastructure: Ideal for Fiber-to-the-Building (FTTB) and distribution point aggregation. Security & Surveillance: Long-haul transmission for high-resolution IP camera networks over distances that exceed multimode limits.

products.title: Fiber Cable 144 Core Single Mode OS2 9/125 GYTS
products.subtitle: Outdoor Fiber Optic Cable, 144 Core, Steel Armored, PE, Jelly filled, GYTS, Single Mode OS2 9/125 (G652D) 10.6mm
products.description: The Netsys NS-401SM144 is an ultra-high-density 144-core OS2 Singlemode fiber optic cable, engineered for massive data throughput over long distances. Featuring a 9/125µm core/cladding diameter, this carrier-grade cable is the definitive solution for core network backbones, metropolitan area networks (MANs), and large-scale data center interconnects where high fiber counts and minimal signal loss are non-negotiable. Technical Specifications: Feature Details Brand/Model Netsys NS-401SM144 Fiber Category OS2 Singlemode (Low Water Peak) Fiber Count 144 Cores Core/Cladding 9/125µm Transmission Distance Up to 10km (10GbE) / Up to 40km+ (Extended) Data Rate Support 10G, 40G, 100G, 400G, and 800G Ethernet Attenuation $\leq 0.35$ dB/km @ 1310nm; $\leq 0.22$ dB/km @ 1550nm Standard Jacket Color Yellow (Standard for OS2) Key Product Features Massive 144-Core Density: Designed to support high-capacity infrastructure, allowing for extensive network branching or high-bandwidth parallel optics in a single, space-saving cable profile. OS2 Singlemode Performance: Optimized for long-haul transmission with "Low Water Peak" characteristics, ensuring stable performance across the entire 1260nm to 1625nm wavelength range. Future-Proof Bandwidth: Unlike Multimode fibers (OM2/OM3/OM4), OS2 has virtually unlimited modal bandwidth, making it compatible with future 400G and 800G networking standards. Loose-Tube Construction: Typically features a loose-tube, gel-filled (or dry-blocked) design to protect the 144 individual fibers from moisture, mechanical stress, and temperature fluctuations. Simplified Identification: Fibers are organized into color-coded sub-units (typically 12 tubes of 12 fibers each) for efficient splicing and cable management at high-density patch panels. Primary Applications Metropolitan & Wide Area Networks (MAN/WAN): Connecting city-wide infrastructures or remote office branches over several kilometers. Hyperscale Data Centers: Serving as the primary spine-and-leaf backbone for massive server farms. ISP & Telecom Infrastructure: Ideal for Fiber-to-the-Home (FTTH) or Fiber-to-the-Building (FTTB) distribution hubs. Campus Core Links: Providing the main high-capacity link between central data centers and various campus buildings.

products.title: Fiber Optic Coupler LC-LC UPC Single Mode Duplex
products.subtitle: The Netsys NS-AD-LCLC-UPC-SM-DX is a high-performance Fiber Optic Coupler (Adapter) used to join two LC duplex fiber patch cables together. It is specifically designed for single-mode networks where signal precision and low data loss are critical.
products.description: The NS-AD-LCLC-UPC-SM-DX represents the high-density standard for modern fiber networking. Utilizing the small form factor (SFF) LC design, this adapter allows for significantly higher port density compared to traditional SC adapters, making it the primary choice for data centers and enterprise-grade infrastructure. Netsys NS-AD-LCLC-UPC-SM-DX The Netsys NS-AD-LCLC-UPC-SM-DX is a high-precision Duplex LC-to-LC Fiber Optic Adapter designed to bridge two pairs of LC fiber connectors. Engineered for Single-Mode (SM) networks with UPC (Ultra Physical Contact) polishing, this adapter provides a compact, reliable, and low-loss connection point within high-density patch panels and networking equipment. Key Features High-Density LC Duplex Design: Features a dual-channel LC-to-LC interface. The LC "small form factor" design effectively doubles the density of your fiber infrastructure compared to SC connectors. Single-Mode Optimized: Precision-engineered for $9/125\mu m$ Single-Mode fiber, ensuring seamless performance for long-haul and high-bandwidth data transmission. Premium Zirconia Ceramic Sleeves: Incorporates high-quality zirconia ceramic alignment sleeves to ensure perfect core-to-core mating, which is critical for maintaining ultra-low insertion loss. UPC Polish Compatible: Designed specifically for blue-coded UPC connectors, ensuring low back reflection and high return loss for sensitive high-speed transceivers. products.description: Product Definition The Grandstream GWN7801P is a Layer 2+ managed enterprise network switch designed for small to medium businesses. It includes 8 Gigabit PoE+ RJ-45 ports and 2 Gigabit SFP uplinks, offering powerful and flexible performance. With a total PoE budget of 120 W, it can power various IP devices such as VoIP phones, cameras, and Wi-Fi access points. The switch supports both local web-based management and cloud/on-premises administration via GWN.Cloud and GWN Manager. Key Use Cases Powering and connecting IP devices without separate power adapters (e.g., VoIP phones, security cameras, Wi-Fi APs). Segmenting network traffic securely via VLANs for voice, data, and guest access. Prioritizing latency-sensitive traffic (voice, video) with advanced QoS features. Centralized provisioning and control across multiple locations using cloud management platforms. Usage Warnings Ensure proper PoE+ (IEEE 802.3at) compliance for devices that need more power. For best results, operate the unit in indoor environments with ambient temperatures between 0 °C and 45 °C. Keep the switch in a dry and dust-controlled environment. Use appropriate ventilation—though cooling is passive, overcrowding racks can cause heat buildup. Technical Specifications Feature Description Layer and Management Layer 2+ with some Layer 3 (ACL routing) capabilities Ports 8 × Gigabit RJ-45 with PoE+; 2 × Gigabit SFP uplinks PoE Standard IEEE 802.3af/at (PoE/PoE+) PoE Budget Up to 30 W per port, 120 W total Switching Capacity 20 Gbps Forwarding Rate 14.88 Mpps MAC Addresses 8K (static, dynamic, filtering, sticky) VLAN 4K VLANs, 802.1Q tagging, voice VLAN, IPvLAN, QinQ QoS Port priority, mapping, queue scheduling: SP, WRR, WFQ, SP-WRR, SP-WFQ Security 802.1X, ACLs (Ethernet and IP), DHCP Snooping, IP Source Guard, ARP Inspection, DoS protection, port isolation, RADIUS/TACACS+ support Multicast IGMP Snooping, MLD Snooping, MVR Management Local Web UI, GWN.Cloud, GWN Manager Dimensions & Weight ~30 × 17.5 × 4.4 cm; weight ~2 kg Environmental Operating: 0 °C to 45 °C; Storage: -10 °C to 60 °C; Humidity: 10-90% non-condensing

products.description: Description GXW4224 Description The Grandstream GXW4224 is a next generation high performance high-density analog VoIP gateway that is fully compliant with SIP standard and interoperable with various VoIP systems, analog PBX and phones on the market. It features multiple FXS analog telephone ports, superb voice quality, rich telephony functionalities, easy provisioning, flexible dialing plans, advanced security protection, and strong performance in handling high volume voice calls. The GXW42XX series gateway offers small and medium businesses a cost-effective hybrid IP and analog telephone system that allows them to enjoy the benefits of VoIP communications while preserving investment on existing analog phones, Fax machines and legacy PBX systems. products.title: HA100 Grandstream automated failover solution for the UCM6510 IP PBX
products.subtitle: The Grandstream HA100 is a High-Availability Controller designed specifically for mission-critical deployments using the UCM6510 IP-PBX . It acts as an automated failover solution, ensuring that if your primary PBX fails, a secondary unit takes over instantly to prevent communication downtime.
products.description: Description Description The HA100 offers an automated failover solution for the UCM6510 IP PBX. When connecting between two UCM6510, the HA100 constantly monitors the operation status of both UCM6510 and automatically switches the system control (including all of the connected telecom lines, network links, auxiliary devices, and all of the SIP endpoints previously registered on the primary UCM6510) to the hot-standby secondary UCM6510 in the event that the primary UCM6510 fails. It can complete the entire system switch between 10 and 50 seconds depending on the number of registered SIP endpoints. Thanks to its smart monitoring and automated failover capability, the HA100 is an ideal high-availability solution for the UCM6510 to ensure maximum total system reliability and uptime. Features Connects and constantly monitors two UCM6510 together for high availability Smart failover solution that automatically switches to a hot-standby secondary UCM6510 if the primary one fails Up to 14 LED indicators showing real-time status of all of the telecom lines, network links, auxiliary devices, etc Gratuitous ARP forces SIP endpoints to refresh the MAC address of the new UCM6510 without interruptions Fast 10 to 50 second system switching time depending on the number of registered endpoints

products.title: Hikvision DS-7604NI-K1 | 4-Channel 4K PoE NVR
products.subtitle: Hikvision NVR supporting 4 channels up to 8MP, equipped with 4 PoE ports for easy camera connectivity, and supports 4K video output.
products.description: Hikvision DS-7604NI-K1 | 4-Channel 4K PoE NVR Next-Generation Surveillance with Ultra-HD 4K Clarity The DS-7604NI-K1 is a professional-grade Network Video Recorder (NVR) designed to deliver crystal-clear security footage. Supporting up to 8MP (4K) resolution , this NVR ensures that every detail—from license plates to facial features—is captured with precision. With its integrated 4-port PoE switch , installation is as simple as plugging in your cameras with a single Ethernet cable for both power and data. Key Features 4K Ultra-HD Output: Supports up to 8MP resolution recording and features an HDMI 4K (3840 × 2160) output , allowing you to view your live feed on high-resolution monitors. Plug & Play PoE: Equipped with 4 independent PoE (Power over Ethernet) interfaces , automatically detecting and configuring Hikvision IP cameras for an instant setup. H.265+ Compression: Uses advanced H.265+ video encoding, which reduces bandwidth and storage requirements by up to 75% without sacrificing video quality. Smart Search & Playback: Save time with intelligent search features that allow you to quickly find specific events within recorded footage. Remote Monitoring: Access your cameras from anywhere in the world using the Hik-Connect app on your smartphone, tablet, or PC. Reliable Storage: Supports one SATA interface for a hard drive up to 8TB , providing weeks of high-definition recording. Technical Specifications Feature Specification IP Video Input 4 Channels Incoming Bandwidth 40 Mbps Decoding Resolution 8MP / 6MP / 5MP / 4MP / 3MP / 1080p / UXGA / 720p Video Output 1-ch HDMI (4K), 1-ch VGA (1080p) PoE Interfaces 4 x RJ45 10/100 Mbps Self-Adaptive SATA Interface 1 SATA Interface (Up to 8TB capacity) USB Interface 2 × USB 2.0 Why Choose the DS-7604NI-K1? "This NVR is the perfect entry point into professional 4K security. It eliminates the need for separate power supplies for your cameras, and Hikvision’s H.265+ technology ensures you don't fill up your hard drive in just a few days." Ideal Use Cases Retail Stores: Monitor checkout counters and entrances with 4K precision to deter theft. Residential Security: Protect your home with a system that is easy to manage via smartphone. Small Offices: Keep an eye on staff and assets with a reliable, 24/7 recording solution.

products.subtitle: Smart-managed Gigabit switch with 24 RJ-45 ports and 4 SFP+ slots, supporting 195W PoE+, ideal for small and medium-sized networks.
products.description: HP Aruba Instant On 1930 JL683A Switch – Professional Performance for Smart Networks The HP Aruba JL683A switch from the Instant On 1930 series is an ideal choice for small and medium-sized businesses seeking reliable performance and easy management. It features 24 RJ-45 ports at 10/100/1000 Mbps and 4 SFP+ ports at 1/10 Gbps, allowing flexible and high-speed connections. With support for PoE+ technology delivering a total power budget of up to 195W, this switch can power devices like wireless access points, surveillance cameras, and VoIP phones without the need for separate power sources. The switch is easily managed via the Aruba Instant On app or through the local web interface, providing flexibility in remote management and monitoring. Key Applications: Small and medium-sized business offices Educational institutions Cafés and restaurants Facilities requiring PoE device connections Usage Warnings: Intended for indoor use only Avoid exceeding the power capacity per port Ensure proper ventilation around the device Technical Specifications: Specification Value Ports 24 × RJ-45 + 4 × SFP+ Speed 10/100/1000 Mbps PoE+ 195W total Switching Capacity 128 Gbps Throughput 95.23 Mpps Memory 512 MB SDRAM, 256 MB Flash Dimensions 44.25 × 26.8 × 4.39 cm Weight 3.47 kg Operating Temperature 0°C to 40°C Operating Humidity 15% to 95%

products.description: HPE Aruba Instant On 1930 JL682A Switch This model is part of the HPE Aruba Instant On 1930 series — a smart-managed Layer 2+ switch designed for small to medium businesses, blending high performance and ease of use. Key Use Cases Deploy fast and reliable connectivity for desktops, printers, and file servers. High-speed uplinks to backbone or data center via SFP+ ports. Flexible management through Instant On mobile app or cloud/web UI with no subscription licenses. Usage Warnings Though designed fanless for quiet operation, ensure adequate ventilation for heat dissipation. Use compatible SFP/SFP+ modules to leverage 10 Gb uplink performance. products.title: HPE Aruba JD120B | X110 100M SFP LC LX Transceiver
products.subtitle: 100M SFP transceiver module with LC connector, supporting single-mode fiber up to 15 km.
products.description: HPE Aruba JD120B | X110 100M SFP LC LX Transceiver Reliable Fiber Connectivity for Long-Distance Networking The HPE Aruba JD120B (X110) is a high-performance Small Form-factor Pluggable (SFP) transceiver designed for 100-Megabit Ethernet applications. Featuring an LC LX interface , this module is specifically engineered for long-reach data transmission over single-mode fiber, making it the perfect choice for connecting switches across different buildings or floors. Key Features Long-Range Performance: Supports data transmission distances of up to 10 kilometers (6.2 miles) over Single-Mode Fiber (SMF). Hot-Swappable Design: Plug-and-play functionality allows you to install or replace the module without powering down your switch, minimizing network downtime. Industry Standard LC Connector: Equipped with a duplex LC connector for secure, standardized fiber optic cabling. Aruba & HPE Certified: Fully tested for seamless compatibility with HPE ProCurve and Aruba switch series, ensuring "Error-Free" operation and full diagnostic support. Low Power Consumption: Optimized for energy efficiency while maintaining high signal integrity. Technical Specifications Feature Specification Model Number JD120B (HPE X110) Data Rate 100 Mbps (Fast Ethernet) Fiber Type Single-Mode Fiber (SMF) Connector Duplex LC Wavelength 1310 nm Max Distance 10 Kilometers Form Factor SFP (Plug-in Module) Compatibility Note This transceiver is a genuine HPE/Aruba product. It is primarily used with Aruba 2530, 2920, and 5400R series switches, as well as legacy HPE ProCurve hardware. Always verify your specific switch's supported SFP list before purchasing. Ideal Use Cases Campus Networking: Linking switches between buildings where distances exceed the 100m limit of copper cabling. Industrial Uplinks: Providing stable 100M connections for remote monitoring stations or IP cameras. Legacy System Support: Maintaining fiber backbones in environments that utilize 100M Fast Ethernet infrastructure.

products.title: HPE Networking Instant On Switch 48p Gigabit CL4 PoE 4p SFP+ 370W 1930
products.subtitle: The HPE Networking Instant On Switch Series 1930 empowers you to do more. Affordable and easy-to-deploy, these smart-managed, fixed-configuration Gigabit switches are designed for the today bandwidth-heavy applications.
products.description: Aruba Instant On 1930 48G Class4 PoE 4SFP/SFP+ 370W Switch (JL686B) Product Overview The Aruba Instant On 1930 series is built for today’s bandwidth-heavy environments. The JL686B model features 48 Gigabit Ethernet ports with Class 4 PoE (PoE+) support, alongside 4 dedicated 10G SFP+ slots for high-speed fiber uplinks. Whether you are powering a fleet of IP cameras, VoIP phones, or Wi-Fi 6 access points, this switch delivers enterprise-grade reliability with simplified, cloud-based management. Key Features High-Power PoE+: 48 ports of IEEE 802.3at Class 4 PoE with a generous 370W power budget , allowing you to power all your edge devices through a single network cable. Lightning-Fast Uplinks: 4 dedicated 1/10G SFP+ ports eliminate network bottlenecks, providing high-speed fiber connectivity to servers or other switches. Management Your Way: Choose between the intuitive Instant On mobile app , the cloud-based web portal, or the local web GUI for advanced configuration. Built-in Security: Protect your business with integrated features like IEEE 802.1X, VLANs, and automatic Denial-of-Service (DoS) protection. Silent & Professional: Sleek 1U rackmount design that fits perfectly into server closets or office environments. Technical Specifications Feature Specification Model Number JL686B (Aruba Instant On 1930 Series) Total Ports 52 (48x RJ45 + 4x SFP/SFP+) RJ45 Port Speed 10/100/1000 Mbps (Gigabit) Uplink Speed 1/10 Gbps SFP+ (Fiber) PoE Budget 370W Class 4 PoE Switching Capacity 176 Gbps Throughput 130.95 Mpps Form Factor 1U Rackmount (Hardware included) Management Cloud Managed (App/Web) or Local Web GUI Ideal For High-Density Offices: Connect and power dozens of workstations, printers, and phones. Security Surveillance: Ideal for large-scale IP camera deployments requiring high PoE wattage. Retail & Hospitality: Seamlessly integrates with Aruba Instant On Access Points for guest Wi-Fi. Tech-Forward SMBs: Professional-grade hardware that doesn't require a dedicated IT department to manage.

This unmanaged switch offers plug-and-play functionality, eliminating the need for complex configurations, making it suitable for environments requiring reliable performance without additional complexities. products.title: Huawei ekit S220-8P4S SWITCH
products.subtitle: S220-8P4S(8*10/100/1000BASE-T ports(125W PoE+),4*GE SFP ports, built-in AC power)
products.description: Description S220-8P4S Overview Table 4-37 Basic information about the S220-8P4S Item Details Description S220-8P4S(8*10/100/1000BASE-T ports(125W PoE+),4*GE SFP ports, built-in AC power) Part Number 98012552 Model S220-8P4S First supported version V600R023C10SPC600 Components Figure 4-11 S220-8P4S appearance 1 One MODE button 2 Eight 10/100/1000BASE-T PoE+ ports 3 Four 1000BASE-X ports 4 One console port 5 One RST button NOTICE: To restore the factory settings and reset the device, hold down the button for at least 6 seconds. To reset the device, press the button. Resetting the device will cause service interruption. Exercise caution when you press the button. 6 AC socket NOTE: It is used with an AC power cable . 7 Jack for AC power cable locking strap NOTE: The AC power cable locking strap is not delivered with the switch. 8 Ground screw NOTE: It is used with a ground cable . Ports Table 4-38 Ports on the S220-8P4S Port Connector Type Description Available Components 10/100/1000BASE-T port RJ45 A 10/100/1000BASE-T Ethernet electrical port sends and receives service data at 10/100/1000 Mbit/s. Ethernet cable 1000BASE-X port SFP A 1000BASE-X port can send and receive data at 1000 Mbit/s. GE eSFP optical modules GE-CWDM eSFP optical modules GE-DWDM eSFP optical modules GE SFP copper module Console port RJ45 The console port is connected to a console for on-site configuration. Console cable Indicators and Buttons Figure 4-12 Indicators on the Switch The S220-8P4S model is used as an example. Non-PoE models do not have PoE indicator and PoE mode. Table 4-39 Description of indicators on the switch No. Indicator Name Color Status Description 1 PWR Power module indicator – Off The switch is powered off. Green Steady on The power supply is normal. Yellow Steady on The power supply is abnormal. 2 SYS System status indicator – Off The system is not running. Green Fast blinking The system is starting. Green Steady on During the system startup preparation phase, the SYS indicator is steady green, which lasts for a maximum of 30 seconds. Green Slow blinking The system is running normally. Red Steady on The system does not work normally after registration, or a fan alarm or a temperature alarm has been generated. 3 PoE PoE indicator – Off The PoE mode is not selected. Green Steady on The PoE mode is selected, and service port indicators show the PoE status of each port. 4 MODE Mode switch button – – When you press this button once, the service port indicators change to the PoE mode and show the PoE status of each service port. When you press this button a second time, the service port indicators restore to the default mode and show the connection status and link activity of each service port. If you do not press the MODE button within 45 seconds, the service port indicators restore to the default mode. In this case, the PoE indicator is off. NOTE: Hold down the mode switch button for 6s and release it to start the web initial login mode. Either of the following situations will occur: If the switch has no configuration file, the system attempts to enter the web initial login mode. If the login fails, check whether the device uses factory default settings. 5 – Service port indicator (one indicator for each port) Arrowheads show the positions of ports. A down arrowhead indicates a port at the bottom, and an up arrowhead indicates a port at the top. Meanings of service port indicators vary in different modes. For details, see Table 4-40 . NOTE: If a power failure occurs on a device’s PCB board, indicators of the last four GE or 10GE optical ports on the device’s front panel blink green cyclically at an interval of 1 second, with each indicator illuminating for 0.25 seconds. 6 CLOUD Cloud indicator NOTE: In versions earlier than V600R024C00, this indicator is reserved. – Off The management VLAN of the device does not obtain an IP address. Blue Steady on The network is connected, and the management VLAN of the device obtains an IP address. Blue Fast blinking The device is connecting to the cloud. Blue Slow blinking The device is in the cloud management state. Table 4-40 Description of service port indicators in different modes (one indicator for each port) Display Mode Color Status Description Default mode – Off The port is not connected or has been shut down. Green Steady on A link has been established on the port. Green Blinking The port is sending or receiving data. PoE mode – Off The port is not providing power to a powered device (PD). Green Steady on The port is providing power to a PD. Green Blinking The power of the PD connected to the port exceeds the power capacity of the port or the power threshold configured on the port. Alternatively, the PD does not comply with IEEE standards. Power Supply System The switch has a built-in AC power module and does not support pluggable power modules. The built-in power module can provide 125 W PoE power, which ensures full PoE power on 8 ports in compliance with 802.3af or on 4 ports in compliance with 802.3at. Heat Dissipation System The switch has a built-in fan for forced air cooling. Air flows in from the left side and front panel, and exhausts from the right side. When working properly at a normal temperature, the device meets the desktop-class noise requirements. However, the fan speed may be high and the noise may be loud during device startup. This figure only shows the airflow direction and does not depict the actual device. Technical Specifications Table 4-41 Technical specifications of the S220-8P4S Item Specification Dimensions without packaging (H x W x D) [mm(in.)] Basic dimensions (excluding the parts protruding from the body): 43.6 mm x 320.0 mm x 210.0 mm (1.72 in. x 12.6 in. x 8.27 in.) Maximum dimensions (the depth is the distance from ports on the front panel to the parts protruding from the rear panel): 43.6 mm x 320.0 mm x 217.0 mm (1.72 in. x 12.6 in. x 8.54 in.) Dimensions with packaging (H x W x D) [mm(in.)] 90.0 mm x 465.0 mm x 380.0 mm (3.54 in. x 18.31 in. x 14.96 in.) products.description: Description S220S-10PN4JX Overview Table 4-21 Basic information about the S220S-10PN4JX Item Details Description S220S-10PN4JX(8*10/100/1000BASE-T ports (6*PoE+), 2*10/100/1000/2.5GBASE-T Ports (PoE+), 2*10GE SFP+ ports, 2*2.5GE SFP ports, 128 W PoE, built-in AC power, Fanless) Part Number 98012475 Model S220S-10PN4JX First supported version V600R024C00 Components Figure 4-10 S220S-10PN4JX appearance 1 One RST button NOTICE: To restore the factory settings and reset the device, hold down the button for at least 6 seconds. To reset the device, press the button. Resetting the device will cause service interruption. Exercise caution when you press the button. 2 One MODE button 3 Six 10/100/1000BASE-T PoE+ ports 4 Two 10/100/1000BASE-T ports 5 Two 10M/100M/1000M/2.5GE BASE-T PoE+ ports (multi-GE ports) 6 Two 10GE SFP+ optical ports 7 Two 2.5GE SFP optical ports 8 AC socket NOTE: It is used with an AC power cable . 9 Jack for AC power cable locking strap NOTE: The AC power cable locking strap is not delivered with the switch. 10 Ground screw NOTE: It is used with a ground cable . Ports Table 4-22 Ports on the S220S-10PN4JX Port Connector Type Description Available Components 10/100/1000BASE-T port RJ45 A 10/100/1000BASE-T Ethernet electrical port sends and receives service data at 10/100/1000 Mbit/s. Ethernet cable 10M/100M/1G/2.5GE BASE-T port (multi-GE port) RJ45 A 10M/100M/1G/2.5GE BASE-T PoE+ port (multi-GE port) sends and receives service data at 10 Mbit/s, 100 Mbit/s, 1 Gbit/s, or 2.5 Gbit/s. If the 2.5 Gbit/s speed is required, the port must use an Ethernet cable of Cat5e or higher category. 2.5GE SFP optical port SFP A 2.5GE SFP Ethernet optical port sends and receives service data at 100 Mbit/s, 1 Gbit/s, or 2.5 Gbit/s. The four optical ports of the device can work at a maximum of two different rates at the same time. If they work at three different rates (10 Gbit/s, 2.5 Gbit/s, and 1000/100 Mbit/s) at the same time, the ports of one rate cannot go Up. FE SFP/eSFP optical modules GE eSFP optical modules GE-CWDM eSFP optical modules GE-DWDM eSFP optical modules 2.5GE eSFP optical modules 10GE SFP+ optical port SFP+ A 10GE SFP+ Ethernet optical port sends and receives service data at 100 Mbit/s, 1 Gbit/s, or 10 Gbit/s. The four optical ports of the device can work at a maximum of two different rates at the same time. If they work at three different rates (10 Gbit/s, 2.5 Gbit/s, and 1000/100 Mbit/s) at the same time, the ports of one rate cannot go Up. FE SFP/eSFP optical modules GE eSFP optical modules GE-CWDM eSFP optical modules GE-DWDM eSFP optical modules 10GE SFP+ optical modules (the maximum transmission distance cannot exceed 10 km) 10GE SFP+ AOC cables Indicators and Buttons Figure 4-11 Indicators on the Switch The S220S-26PN4JX model is used as an example. Table 4-23 Description of indicators on the switch No. Indicator Name Color Status Description 1 PWR Power module indicator – Off The switch is powered off. Green Steady on The power supply is normal. 2 SYS System status indicator – Off The system is not running. Green Fast blinking The system is starting. Green Steady on During the system startup preparation phase, the SYS indicator is steady green, which lasts for a maximum of 30 seconds. Green Slow blinking The system is running normally. Red Steady on The system does not work normally after registration, or a fan alarm or a temperature alarm has been generated. 3 CLOUD Cloud indicator – Off The management VLAN of the device does not obtain an IP address. Blue Steady on The network is connected, and the management VLAN of the device obtains an IP address. Blue Fast blinking The device is connecting to the cloud. Blue Slow blinking The device is in the cloud management state. 4 PoE PoE indicator – Off The PoE mode is not selected. Green Steady on The PoE mode is selected, and service port indicators show the PoE status of each port. 5 MODE Mode switch button – – When you press this button once (hold it down for more than 1 second), the service port indicators change to the PoE mode and show the PoE status of each service port. When you press this button a second time (hold it down for more than 1 second), the service port indicators restore to the default mode and show the connection status and link activity of each service port. If you do not press the MODE button within 45 seconds, the service port indicators restore to the default mode. In this case, the PoE indicator is off. 6 – Service port indicator (one indicator for each port) Arrowheads show the positions of ports. A down arrowhead indicates a port at the bottom, and an up arrowhead indicates a port at the top. Meanings of service port indicators vary in different modes. For details, see Table 4-24 . Table 4-24 Description of service port indicators in different modes (one indicator for each port) Display Mode Color Status Description Default mode – Off The port is not connected or has been shut down. Green Steady on A link has been established on the port. Green Blinking The port is sending or receiving data. PoE mode – Off The port is not providing power to a powered device (PD). Green Steady on The port is providing power to a PD. Green Blinking The power of the PD connected to the port exceeds the power capacity of the port or the power threshold configured on the port. Alternatively, the PD does not comply with IEEE standards. Power Supply System The switch has a built-in AC power module and does not support pluggable power modules. The built-in power module can provide 128 W PoE power, which ensures full PoE power on 8 ports in compliance with 802.3af or on 4 ports in compliance with 802.3at. Heat Dissipation System The device has no fans and uses natural heat dissipation. Technical Specifications Table 4-25 Technical specifications of the S220S-10PN4JX Item Specification Dimensions without packaging (H x W x D) [mm(in.)] Basic dimensions (excluding the parts protruding from the body): 43.6 mm x 320 mm x 210 mm (1.72 in. x 12.60 in. x 8.27 in.) Maximum dimensions (the depth is the distance from ports on the front panel to the parts protruding from the rear panel): 43.6 mm x 320 mm x 217 mm (1.72 in. x 12.60 in. x 8.54 in.) Dimensions with packaging (H x W x D) [mm(in.)] 91.0 mm x 465.0 mm x 355.0 mm (3.58 in. x 18.31 in. x 13.98 in.) Chassis height [U] 1 U Chassis material Metal Weight without packaging [kg(lb)] 2.55 kg (5.62 lb) Weight with packaging [kg(lb)] 3.44 kg (7.58 lb) Typical power consumption [W] 23.57 W <td class="idp-ltr-html-cellrowborder" he

products.description: Description S220S-26PN4JX Overview Table 4-49 Basic information about the S220S-26PN4JX Item Details Description S220S-26PN4JX (24*10/100/1000BASE-T ports (22*PoE+), 2*10/100/1000/2.5GBASE-T Combo (PoE+), 2*10GE SFP+ ports, 400 W PoE, built-in AC power) Part Number 98012521 Model S220S-26PN4JX First supported version V600R024C00 Remarks If the upper-level circuit breaker for a device is also connected to many other devices, a C32 or above circuit breaker needs to be used. Components Figure 4-22 S220S-26PN4JX appearance 1 One RST button NOTICE: To restore the factory settings and reset the device, hold down the button for at least 6 seconds. The two ports with the same port number cannot be used at the same time. In the default working mode, the electrical ports in the combo ports are available. 6 Two 10GE SFP+ optical ports 7 Two 2.5GE SFP optical ports NOTE: The 10M/100M/1G/2.5GE BASE-T port and 2.5GE SFP optical port are combo ports. The two ports with the same port number cannot be used at the same time. In the default working mode, the electrical ports in the combo ports are available. 8 Ground screw NOTE: It is used with a ground cable . 9 Jack for AC power cable locking strap NOTE: The AC power cable locking strap is not delivered with the switch. 10 AC socket NOTE: It is used with an AC power cable . Ports Table 4-50 Ports on the S220S-26PN4JX Port Connector Type Description Available Components 10/100/1000BASE-T port RJ45 A 10/100/1000BASE-T Ethernet electrical port sends and receives service data at 10/100/1000 Mbit/s. Ethernet cable 10M/100M/1G/2.5GE BASE-T port (multi-GE port) RJ45 A 10M/100M/1G/2.5GE BASE-T PoE+ port (multi-GE port) sends and receives service data at 10 Mbit/s, 100 Mbit/s, 1 Gbit/s, or 2.5 Gbit/s. The 10M/100M/1G/2.5GE BASE-T electrical port and 2.5GE SFP optical port are combo ports. A combo port refers to a pair of ports consisting of an optical Ethernet port and an electrical Ethernet port on the panel. Each combo port matches only one internal forwarding port. A combo port can be configured as an electrical port or an optical port, but only one port can be active at a time. When one port is active, the other port is shut down. The two 10GE optical ports and two combo ports of the device can work at a maximum of two different rates at the same time. If they work at three different rates (10 Gbit/s, 2.5 Gbit/s, and 1000/100/10 Mbit/s) at the same time, the ports of one rate cannot go Up. If the 2.5 Gbit/s speed is required, the port must use an Ethernet cable of Cat5e or higher category. 2.5GE SFP optical port SFP A 2.5GE SFP Ethernet optical port sends and receives service data at 100 Mbit/s, 1 Gbit/s, or 2.5 Gbit/s. The 10M/100M/1G/2.5GE BASE-T electrical port and 2.5GE SFP optical port are combo ports. A combo port refers to a pair of ports consisting of an optical Ethernet port and an electrical Ethernet port on the panel. Each combo port matches only one internal forwarding port. A combo port can be configured as an electrical port or an optical port, but only one port can be active at a time. When one port is active, the other port is shut down. The two 10GE optical ports and two combo ports of the device can work at a maximum of two different rates at the same time. If they work at three different rates (10 Gbit/s, 2.5 Gbit/s, and 1000/100 Mbit/s) at the same time, the ports of one rate cannot go Up. FE SFP/eSFP optical modules GE eSFP optical modules GE-CWDM eSFP optical modules GE-DWDM eSFP optical modules 2.5GE eSFP optical modules 10GE SFP+ optical port SFP+ A 10GE SFP+ Ethernet optical port sends and receives service data at 100 Mbit/s, 1 Gbit/s, or 10 Gbit/s. The two 10GE optical ports and two combo ports of the device can work at a maximum of two different rates at the same time. If they work at three different rates (10 Gbit/s, 2.5 Gbit/s, and 1000/100 Mbit/s) at the same time, the ports of one rate cannot go Up. FE SFP/eSFP optical modules GE eSFP optical modules GE-CWDM eSFP optical modules GE-DWDM eSFP optical modules 10GE SFP+ optical modules 10GE SFP+ AOC cables Indicators and Buttons Figure 4-23 Indicators on the Switch The S220S-26PN4JX model is used as an example. Table 4-51 Description of indicators on the switch No. Indicator Name Color Status Description 1 PWR Power module indicator – Off The switch is powered off. Green Steady on The power supply is normal. 2 SYS System status indicator – Off The system is not running. Green Fast blinking The system is starting. Green Steady on During the system startup preparation phase, the SYS indicator is steady green, which lasts for a maximum of 30 seconds. Green Slow blinking The system is running normally. Red Steady on The system does not work normally after registration, or a fan alarm or a temperature alarm has been generated. 3 CLOUD Cloud indicator – Off The management VLAN of the device does not obtain an IP address. Blue Steady on The network is connected, and the management VLAN of the device obtains an IP address. Blue Fast blinking The device is connecting to the cloud. Blue Slow blinking The device is in the cloud management state. 4 PoE PoE indicator – Off The PoE mode is not selected. Green Steady on The PoE mode is selected, and service port indicators show the PoE status of each port. 5 MODE Mode switch button – – When you press this button once (hold it down for more than 1 second), the service port indicators change to the PoE mode and show the PoE status of each service port. When you press this button a second time (hold it down for more than 1 second), the service port indicators restore to the default mode and show the connection status and link activity of each service port. If you do not press the MODE button within 45 seconds, the service port indicators restore to the default mode. In this case, the PoE indicator is off. 6 – Service port indicator (one indicator for each port) Arrowheads show the positions of ports. A down arrowhead indicates a port at the bottom, and an up arrowhead indicates a port at the top. Meanings of service port indicators vary in different modes. For details, see Table 4-52 . Table 4-52 Description of service port indicators in different modes (one indicator for each port) Display Mode Color Status Description Default mode – Off The port is not connected or has been shut down. Green Steady on A link has been established on the port. Green Blinking The port is sending or receiving data. PoE mode – Off The port is not providing power to a powered device (PD). Green Steady on The port is providing power to a PD. Green Blinking The power of the PD connected to the port exceeds the power capacity of the port or the power threshold configured on the port. Alternatively, the PD does not comply with IEEE standards. Power Supply System The switch has a built-in AC power module and does not support pluggable power modules. The built-in power module can provide 400 W PoE power, which ensures full PoE power on 24 ports in compliance with 802.3af or on 13 ports in compliance with 802.3at. Heat Dissipation System The switch has two built-in fans for forced air cooling. Air flows in from the left side and front panel, and exhausts from the right side. When working properly at a normal temperature, the device meets the desktop-class noise requirements. However, the fan speed may be high and the noise may be loud during device startup.

products.description: Description S310-24ST4X Overview Table 4-101 Basic information about the S310-24ST4X Item Details Description S310-24ST4X(24*GE SFP ports, 8 of which are dual-purpose 10/100/1000 or SFP, 4*10GE SFP+ ports, built-in AC power) Part Number 98012532 Model S310-24ST4X First supported version V600R023C10SPC600 Components Figure 4-35 S310-24ST4X appearance 1 One MODE button 2 Sixteen 100/1000BASE-X ports 3 Eight Combo ports (100/1000BASE-X optical ports and 10/100/1000BASE-T electrical ports) NOTE: In the default working mode, the electrical ports in the combo ports are available. 4 Four 10GE SFP+ ports 5 One console port 6 One ETH management port 7 One RST button NOTICE: To restore the factory settings and reset the device, hold down the button for at least 6 seconds. To reset the device, press the button. Resetting the device will cause service interruption. Exercise caution when you press the button. 8 Ground screw NOTE: It is used with a ground cable . 9 Jack for AC power cable locking strap NOTE: The AC power cable locking strap is not delivered with the switch. 10 AC socket NOTE: It is used with an AC power cable . Ports Table 4-102 Ports on the S310-24ST4X Port Connector Type Description Available Components 100/1000BASE-X port SFP A 100/1000BASE-X port can send and receive data at 100/1000 Mbit/s. FE SFP/eSFP optical modules GE eSFP optical modules GE-CWDM eSFP optical modules GE-DWDM eSFP optical modules GE SFP copper module Combo port (10/100/1000BASE-T + 100/1000BASE-X) RJ45/SFP A combo port refers to a pair of ports consisting of an optical Ethernet port and an electrical Ethernet port on the panel. Each combo port matches only one internal forwarding port. A combo port can be configured as an electrical port or an optical port, but only one port can be active at a time. When one port is active, the other port is shut down. Ethernet cable FE SFP/eSFP optical modules GE eSFP optical modules GE-CWDM eSFP optical modules GE-DWDM eSFP optical modules 10GE SFP+ optical port SFP+ A 10GE SFP+ Ethernet optical port supports auto-sensing to 1000 Mbit/s. It sends and receives service data at 1000 Mbit/s or 10 Gbit/s. GE eSFP optical modules GE-CWDM eSFP optical modules GE-DWDM eSFP optical modules GE SFP copper module 10GE SFP+ optical modules 10GE-CWDM SFP+ optical modules 10GE-DWDM SFP+ optical modules 1 m, 2 m, and 3 m SFP+ high-speed copper cables 10 m SFP+ AOC cables 0.5 m and 1.5 m SFP+ dedicated stack cables (only for zero-configuration stacking) Console port RJ45 The console port is connected to a console for on-site configuration. Console cable ETH management port RJ45 You can connect a switch to a configuration terminal or network management workstation through the ETH management port to configure the switch locally or remotely. Ethernet cable Indicators and Buttons Figure 4-36 Indicators on the Switch Table 4-103 Description of indicators on the switch No. Indicator Name Color Status Description 1 PWR Power module indicator – Off The switch is powered off. Green Steady on The power supply is normal. 2 SYS System status indicator – Off The system is not running. Green Fast blinking The system is starting. Green Steady on During the system startup preparation phase, the SYS indicator is steady green, which lasts for a maximum of 30 seconds. Green Slow blinking The system is running normally. Red Steady on The system does not work normally after registration, or a fan alarm or a temperature alarm has been generated. 3 MST Stack indicator – Off If you are not changing the indicator mode (default): The switch is a standby or slave switch in a stack or the stacking function is not enabled on the switch. If you are changing the indicator mode: The stack mode is not selected. Green Steady on The stack mode is selected. The switch is a standby or slave switch in a stack, and the service port indicators show the stack ID of the switch. Green Blinking If you are not changing the indicator mode (default): The switch is the master switch in a stack or a standalone switch with the stacking function enabled. If you are changing the indicator mode: The stack mode is selected. The switch is the master switch in a stack or a standalone switch, and the service port indicators show the stack ID of the master switch. After 45 seconds, the service port indicators automatically restore to the status mode. 4 MODE Mode switch button – – When you press this button once, the service port indicators change to the stack mode and show the stack ID of the local switch. When you press this button a second time, the service port indicators restore to the default mode and show the connection status and link activity of each service port. If you do not press the MODE button within 45 seconds, the service port indicators restore to the default mode. NOTE: Hold down the mode switch button for 6s and release it to start the web initial login mode. Either of the following situations will occur: If the switch has no configuration file, the system attempts to enter the web initial login mode. If the system enters the web initial login mode successfully, the MST mode indicator turn green and stay on for a maximum of 45 seconds. If the login fails, check whether the device uses factory default settings. 5 CLOUD Cloud indicator NOTE: In versions earlier than V600R024C00, this indicator is reserved. – Off The management VLAN of the device does not obtain an IP address. Blue Steady on The network is connected, and the management VLAN of the device obtains an IP address. Blue Fast blinking The device is connecting to the cloud. Blue Slow blinking The device is in the cloud management state. 6 – Optical service port indicator (two indicators for each port) Each optical port has two single-color indicators. The one on the left is the ACT indicator (yellow), and the one on the right is the LINK indicator (green). Arrowheads show the positions of ports. A down arrowhead indicates a port at the bottom, and an up arrowhead indicates a port at the top. Meanings of service port indicators vary in different modes. For details, see Table 4-104 and Table 4-105 . NOTE: If a power failure occurs on a device’s PCB board, indicators of the last four GE or 10GE optical ports on the device’s front panel blink green cyclically at an interval of 1 second, with each indicator illuminating for 0.25 seconds. 7 – Electrical or optical service port indicator (one indicator for each port) Arrowheads show the positions of ports. A down arrowhead indicates a port at the bottom, and an up arrowhead indicates a port at the top. 8 ETH ETH port indicator – <td class="idp-ltr-html-cellrowborder" head

products.title: Huawei ekit S310S-24T4J SWITCH
products.subtitle: S310S-24T4J (24*10/100/1000BASE-T ports, 4*2.5GE SFP ports, built-in AC power, Fanless)
products.description: Here is a professional product description for the Huawei S310S-24T4J switch, formatted to help your customers understand its value quickly. Huawei S310S-24T4J | CloudEngine S310S Series Switch The Huawei S310S-24T4J is a high-performance, next-generation Gigabit Ethernet switch designed for small to medium-sized enterprise (SME) networks, internet service providers (ISPs), and branch offices. 1 Built on Huawei’s high-performance hardware platform, it provides reliable, secure, and energy-efficient switching capabilities. Key Features High-Density Connectivity: Equipped with 24 × 10/100/1000Base-T ports to support a wide range of wired devices. 2 Flexible Uplinks: Features 4 × Gigabit SFP ports , allowing for high-speed fiber uplinks to aggregation or core switches. 3 Silent & Energy Efficient: Designed for low power consumption and quiet operation, making it ideal for office environments where noise sensitivity is a priority. Intelligent Management: Supports simplified O&M (Operations and Maintenance) through Huawei’s eSight and web-based management interfaces. 4 Enhanced Security: Includes robust security features such as ACLs, MAC address authentication, and 802.1X to protect your network from unauthorized access. Technical Specifications Feature Specification Downlink Ports 24 x 10/100/1000Base-T Uplink Ports 4 x GE SFP Forwarding Performance 42 Mpps Switching Capacity 56 Gbps MAC Address Table 8K VLAN Support 4094 VLANs Form Factor 1U Rack-mountable

Ports Table 4-59 Ports on the S310S-8P4JX Port Connector Type Description Available Components 10/100/1000BASE-T port RJ45 A 10/100/1000BASE-T Ethernet electrical port sends and receives service data at 10/100/1000 Mbit/s. Ethernet cable 2.5GE SFP optical port SFP A 2.5GE SFP Ethernet optical port sends and receives service data at 100 Mbit/s, 1 Gbit/s, or 2.5 Gbit/s. The four optical ports of the device can work at a maximum of two different rates at the same time. If they work at three different rates (10 Gbit/s, 2.5 Gbit/s, and 1000/100 Mbit/s) at the same time, the ports of one rate cannot go Up. FE SFP/eSFP optical modules GE eSFP optical modules GE-CWDM eSFP optical modules GE-DWDM eSFP optical modules 2.5GE eSFP optical modules 10GE SFP+ optical port SFP+ A 10GE SFP+ Ethernet optical port sends and receives service data at 100 Mbit/s, 1 Gbit/s, or 10 Gbit/s. The four optical ports of the device can work at a maximum of two different rates at the same time. If they work at three different rates (10 Gbit/s, 2.5 Gbit/s, and 1000/100 Mbit/s) at the same time, the ports of one rate cannot go Up. FE SFP/eSFP optical modules GE eSFP optical modules GE-CWDM eSFP optical modules GE-DWDM eSFP optical modules 10GE SFP+ optical modules (the maximum transmission distance cannot exceed 10 km) 10GE SFP+ AOC cables Indicators and Buttons Figure 4-27 Indicators on the Switch The S310S-26PN4JX model is used as an example. Non-PoE models do not have PoE indicator and PoE mode. Table 4-60 Description of indicators on the switch No. Indicator Name Color Status Description 1 PWR Power module indicator - Off The switch is powered off. Green Steady on The power supply is normal. 2 SYS System status indicator - Off The system is not running. Green Fast blinking The system is starting. Green Steady on During the system startup preparation phase, the SYS indicator is steady green, which lasts for a maximum of 30 seconds. Green Slow blinking The system is running normally. Red Steady on The system does not work normally after registration, or a fan alarm or a temperature alarm has been generated. 3 CLOUD Cloud indicator - Off The device is not connected to the cloud. Blue Fast blinking The device is connecting to the cloud. Blue Slow blinking The device is in the cloud management state. 4 PoE PoE indicator - Off The PoE mode is not selected. Green Steady on The PoE mode is selected, and service port indicators show the PoE status of each port. 5 MODE Mode switch button - - When you press this button once (hold it down for more than 1 second), the service port indicators change to the PoE mode and show the PoE status of each service port. When you press this button a second time (hold it down for more than 1 second), the service port indicators restore to the default mode and show the connection status and link activity of each service port. If you do not press the MODE button within 45 seconds, the service port indicators restore to the default mode. In this case, the PoE indicator is off. 6 - Service port indicator (one indicator for each port) Arrowheads show the positions of ports. A down arrowhead indicates a port at the bottom, and an up arrowhead indicates a port at the top. Meanings of service port indicators vary in different modes. For details, see Table 4-61 . Table 4-61 Description of service port indicators in different modes (one indicator for each port) Display Mode Color Status Description Default mode - Off The port is not connected or has been shut down. Green Steady on A link has been established on the port. Green Blinking The port is sending or receiving data. PoE mode - Off The port is not providing power to a powered device (PD). Green Steady on The port is providing power to a PD. Green Blinking The power of the PD connected to the port exceeds the power capacity of the port or the power threshold configured on the port. Alternatively, the PD does not comply with IEEE standards. Power Supply System The switch has a built-in AC power module and does not support pluggable power modules. The device provides 8 PoE ports. The maximum output power of a PoE port is 30 W, and the maximum PoE output power of the entire device is 128 W. Heat Dissipation System The device has no fans and uses natural heat dissipation. products.title: Huawei Ekit S380-L4P1T Multi Service Gateway
products.subtitle: The Huawei S380-L4P1T is a compact, unmanaged Gigabit Ethernet switch designed specifically for small businesses, home offices (SOHO), and distributed edge connectivity. Part of the Huawei CloudEngine S380 series, it focuses on extreme simplicity, reliability, and efficient power delivery in a "mini" form factor.
products.description: Overview Table 4-6 Basic information about the S380-L4P1T Item Details Description S380-L4P1T, 1*GE WAN, 4*GE LAN(PoE+, 50W) Part Number 98012178 Model S380-L4P1T First supported version V600R022C10 Components Figure 4-3 S380-L4P1T appearance 1 CLOUD indicator 2 SYS indicator 3 One RST button NOTICE: To restore the factory settings and reset the device, hold down the button for at least 6 seconds. To reset the device, press the button. Resetting the device will cause service interruption. Exercise caution when you press the button. 4 Three GE PoE+ LAN electrical ports 5 One GE PoE+ LAN/WAN electrical port 6 One GE WAN electrical port 7 Port indicator 8 Port indicator status switch button NOTE: Port status: The port indicator indicates the data transmission status of the port. PoE status: The port indicator indicates the PoE status of the port. 9 Power adapter socket NOTE: Use the power adapter (56 V 1.07 A) delivered with the device. 10 Ground screw Ports Table 4-7 Ports on the S380-L4P1T Port Connector Type Description Available Components GE PoE+ LAN electrical port RJ45 A GE PoE+ LAN electrical port sends and receives LAN service data at 10/100/1000 Mbit/s. The port supports PoE function. Ethernet cable GE WAN electrical port RJ45 A GE WAN electrical port sends and receives WAN service data at 10/100/1000 Mbit/s. Ethernet cable GE PoE+ LAN/WAN electrical port RJ45 A GE PoE+ LAN/WAN electrical port sends and receives LAN or WAN service data at 10/100/1000 Mbit/s. The port supports PoE function. Ethernet cable Indicators and Buttons Table 4-8 Description of indicators on the device Indicator Name Color Status Description SYS System status indicator - Off The system is not running or is in the reset state. Green Fast blinking The system is in the power-on loading or reset startup state. Green Slow blinking The system is running normally. Green Steady on During the system startup preparation phase, the SYS indicator is steady green, which lasts for a maximum of 15 seconds. Red Steady on The device cannot be started or the power supply is faulty. Cloud Cloud indicator - Off The device is not in the cloud management state. Blue Fast blinking The device is connecting to the cloud. Blue Slow blinking The device is in the cloud management state. Blue Steady on The network is connected, and the WAN port obtains an IP address. NOTE: This function is available in V600R023C00 and later versions. - Port indicator (Port status) - Off The port is not connected or has been shut down. Green Steady on A link has been established on the port. NOTE: During device startup, all port indicators are steady green for about 2 seconds and then off, indicating that service initialization is complete. Green Blinking The port is sending or receiving data. Port indicator (PoE status) - Off The port is not supplying PoE power. Green Steady on The port is supplying power to the connected PD. Green Blinking The PoE power of the device is insufficient, and the port cannot provide power to the PD. Power Supply System The device uses the power adapter delivered with the device to supply power to the device and the connected PD. The power adapter can provide 50 W PoE power, which ensures full PoE power on 3 ports in compliance with 802.3af or on 1 ports in compliance with 802.3at. Heat Dissipation System The device has no fans and uses natural heat dissipation. products.title: Huawei eKitEngine AP361 Wi-Fi 6 Access Point
products.subtitle: Indoor Wi-Fi 6 access point delivering up to 1.775 Gbps, featuring built-in smart antennas and cloud-based network management support.
products.description: Product Overview The Huawei eKitEngine AP361 is an indoor Wi-Fi 6 (802.11ax) access point designed to deliver high-performance wireless coverage in small to medium-sized enterprise environments. Key Applications Small and medium-sized offices Educational institutions Hospitals and clinics Hotels and cafés Usage Warnings For indoor use only Requires a PoE power source (IEEE 802.3af compliant) Avoid exposure to moisture or temperatures outside the recommended range Technical Specifications Feature Description Wireless Standards 802.11a/b/g/n/ac/ax Frequency Bands 2.4 GHz and 5 GHz Antennas Built-in smart antennas (4 total) Maximum Data Rate 1.775 Gbps Concurrent Users Up to 100 Network Port 1 × GE RJ45 supporting PoE Maximum Power Consumption 8.8 W Dimensions 180 mm × 35 mm Weight 0.45 kg Operating Temperature 0°C to +50°C Management Methods Huawei eKit App, Fit/Fat modes, Cloud management Security Options WPA3, WPA2-PSK, 802.1X, WIDS/WIPS

products.title: Huawei eKitEngine AP362E Wireless Access Point
products.subtitle: Huawei eKitEngine AP362E is an indoor access point (AP) in compliance with the Wi-Fi 6 (802.11ax) standard. It provides services simultaneously on the 2.4 GHz (2x2 MIMO) and 5 GHz (2x2 MIMO) frequency bands, delivering a data rate of up to 2.975 Gbps.
products.description: Huawei AirEngine AP362E | Wi-Fi 6 Wall-Plate Access Point Superior Wi-Fi 6 performance delivered in a compact, space-saving design. The AirEngine AP362E is part of Huawei's next-generation wireless portfolio, offering high-speed dual-band connectivity. By supporting the latest Wi-Fi 6 standards, it delivers significantly higher throughput and lower latency compared to traditional Wi-Fi 5 models. Its wall-plate design allows it to be installed directly into standard electrical junction boxes, providing a clean "hidden" look that blends into any room decor. Key Features Wi-Fi 6 (802.11ax) Standard: Supports 2x2 MIMO on both the 2.4 GHz and 5 GHz bands, achieving a combined theoretical rate of up to 1.775 Gbps . Smart Antenna Technology: Automatically adjusts the signal direction to follow the user, ensuring stable coverage and minimizing interference. Compact Wall-Plate Design: Fits seamlessly into standard 86mm, 118mm, or 120mm wall boxes. It’s an ideal "set and forget" solution that doesn't take up desk or ceiling space. Versatile Connectivity: Features a front-facing 10/100/1000Mbps Ethernet port for wired guest access and a pass-through RJ45 port for telephone or legacy data lines. Cloud-Managed Efficiency: Fully compatible with Huawei’s iMaster NCE-Campus , allowing for simplified deployment, remote configuration, and AI-powered network optimization. Power over Ethernet (PoE): Simplifies installation by drawing power and data through a single Ethernet cable (802.3af/at compliant). Technical Specifications Feature Specification Model AirEngine AP362E Wi-Fi Standard Wi-Fi 6 (802.11ax/ac/n/g/b/a) Max Data Rate 1.775 Gbps (575Mbps @ 2.4GHz + 1.2Gbps @ 5GHz) Uplink Port 1 x 10/100/1000M Base-T (PoE In) Service Ports 1 x 10/100/1000M Base-T (Front) Antenna Type Built-in Smart Antennas Dimensions 86 x 86 x 33 mm (Standard Wall Plate) Operating Temp 0°C to 40°C

products.title: Huawei eKitEngine AP371 Wi-Fi 7  Access Point
products.subtitle: The Huawei eKitEngine AP371 is a next-generation indoor Access Point that brings the power of Wi-Fi 7 (802.11be) to small and medium-sized enterprises. It is designed to handle high-density environments with extreme speed, ultra-low latency, and intelligent signal management.
products.description: Overview Table 4-16 Basic information about the AP371 Item Details Description AP371(11be indoor,2+2 dual bands,smart antenna,USB) Part Number 50087399-001 Model AP371 First supported version V600R023C10 Appearance Figure 4-10 Appearance of the AP371 Ports Figure 4-11 Ports on the AP371 1 DC12V 2 2.5GE/PoE_IN 3 USB 4 Security slot 5 Management console port - - Table 4-17 Ports on the AP371 Port Connector Type Description Available Components 2.5GE/PoE_IN RJ45 100M/1000M/2.5G auto-sensing Ethernet electrical port that connects to the wired Ethernet and supports PoE input. Network cable DC 12V DC connector Connects to a 12 V power adapter. 12 V DC power adapter USB USB Type A Provides 2.5 W or 5 W power supply. - Management console port Pin header socket Management console port, allowing operations only by professionals - Indicators and Buttons Figure 4-12 Indicators and buttons on the AP371 1 Indicator 2 Default Table 4-18 Indicators on the AP371 Silkscreen Name Color Status Description - System indicator - Off The system is not running. Green Steady on The system is just powered on. The system is starting after a reset. The upper-layer system is starting. Green Steady on after blinking once After the hardware reset is cleared and the software starts, the indicator blinks once. Then, the indicator is steady on until the bottom-layer system starts. Green Slow blinking (0.5 Hz) The AP is running properly, the Ethernet connection is normal, and STAs are associated with the AP. Green Slow blinking (0.2 Hz) The AP is running properly, the Ethernet connection is normal, and no STA is associated with the AP. Green Blinking once every 0.25s (4 Hz) The bottom-layer system is being started. The software is being upgraded. After the software is loaded and started, the AP requests to go online in Fit or cloud management mode. The indicator remains in this state till the AP successfully goes online. Yellow (blinking red and green at the same time, causing color deviation) Blinking once every 0.25s (4 Hz) Hold down the Default button for more than 6 seconds to restore the factory settings. This function is supported in V600R024C10. Red Blinking once every 0.25s (4 Hz) Hold down the Default button for more than 6 seconds to restore the factory settings. This function is supported in V600R25C00 or later. Red Steady on A fault that affects services has occurred, such as a DRAM detection failure or system software loading failure. The fault cannot be automatically rectified and must be rectified manually. Table 4-19 Buttons on the AP371 Silkscreen Name Description Default Reset button For versions from V600R023C10 to V600R024C00: If you press the button, the device resets. If you hold down the button for more than 6 seconds, the device restores the factory settings, switches to the Fit mode, and restarts. For V600R024C10: If you press the button, the device resets. If you hold down the button for more than 6 seconds, the indicator blinks yellow (blinking red and green fast at the same time, causing color deviation), and the device restores the factory settings, switches to the Fit mode, and restarts. For V600R025C00 and later versions: If you press the button, the device resets. If you hold down the button for more than 6 seconds, the indicator blinks red fast and the device restores the factory settings, switches to the Fit mode, and restarts. products.title: Huawei eKitEngine S110-24LP2SR Unmanaged 24-Port Gigabit PoE+ Switch with 2 SFP Ports
products.subtitle: Huawei unmanaged switch featuring 24 Gigabit PoE+ ports and 2 SFP ports, ideal for small to medium networks.
products.description: Overview Table 4-32 Basic information about the S110-24LP2SR Item Details Description S110-24LP2SR (24*10/100/1000BASE-T ports, 2*GE SFP ports, PoE+, AC power) Part Number 98012198 Model S110-24LP2SR Components Figure 4-9 S110-24LP2SR appearance 1 PWR indicator 2 Twenty-four GE PoE+ electrical ports NOTE: Only the new model with the Forwarding First and PoE First silkscreens on the front panel supports the following functions: Forwarding First: This function is available on ports 1 to 8. With this function, traffic on ports 1 to 8 is preferentially forwarded when traffic congestion occurs on uplink ports. PoE First: This function is available on ports 1 to 8. With this function, terminals connected to other ports will not preempt the power of ports 1 to 8 when the device cannot supply full PoE power to connected terminals; additionally, the power of these ports will be preferentially restored after the device restarts. 3 Electrical port indicator 4 Two GE optical ports 5 Optical port indicator 6 Port mode switch button NOTE: Standard Mode: All ports are in the same VLAN, and the flow control function is enabled. Port Isolation: All downlink ports are isolated from each other and cannot communicate with each other. Downlink ports can communicate only with uplink ports. (The last two downlink electrical ports are not isolated.) Uplink ports are not isolated or aggregated. Uplink Aggregation: Two uplink optical ports are aggregated into a trunk interface. Eth-Trunk supports load balancing only based on source physical ports. Flow Control Disabling: All ports are in the same VLAN and flow control is disabled. 7 Port indicator status switch button NOTE: Port status: The port indicator indicates the data transmission status of the port. PoE status: The port indicator indicates the PoE status of the port. 8 Ground screw 9 AC socket NOTE: Use the power cable delivered with the device. - - Ports Table 4-33 Ports on the S110-24LP2SR Port Connector Type Description Available Components GE PoE+ electrical port RJ45 A GE PoE+ electrical port sends and receives service data at 10/100/1000 Mbit/s. The port supports the PoE function. Ethernet cable GE optical port SFP A GE optical port can send and receive data at 100/1000 Mbit/s. FE SFP/eSFP optical modules (only optical modules with transmission distances less than or equal to 15 km are supported) GE eSFP optical modules (only optical modules with transmission distances less than or equal to 10 km are supported) Indicators and Buttons Table 4-34 Description of indicators on the device Indicator Color Status Description PWR indicator - Off The device is powered off. Green Steady on The power supply is normal. Port indicator (Port status) - Off The port is not connected or has been shut down. Green Steady on A link has been established on the port. NOTE: During device startup, all port indicators are steady green for about 2 seconds and then off, indicating that service initialization is complete. Green Blinking The port is sending or receiving data. Port indicator (PoE status) - Off The port is not supplying PoE power. Green Steady on The port is supplying power to the connected PD. Green Blinking The PoE power of the device is insufficient, and the port cannot provide power to the PD. Power Supply System The device uses the power adapter delivered with the device to supply power to the device and the connected PD. The device provides 24 PoE ports. The maximum output power of a PoE port is 30 W, and the maximum PoE output power of the entire device is 124 W. Heat Dissipation System The device has no fans and uses natural heat dissipation. Table 4-127 Description of indicators on the switch No. Indicator Name Color Status Description 1 PWR Power module indicator - Off The switch is powered off. Green Steady on The power supply is normal. Yellow Steady on The power supply is abnormal. 2 SYS System status indicator - Off The system is not running. Green Fast blinking The system is starting. Green Steady on During the system startup preparation phase, the SYS indicator is steady green, which lasts for a maximum of 30 seconds. Green Slow blinking The system is running normally. Red Steady on The system does not work normally after registration, or a fan alarm or a temperature alarm has been generated. 3 MST Stack indicator - Off If you are not changing the indicator mode (default): The switch is a standby or slave switch in a stack or the stacking function is not enabled on the switch. If you are changing the indicator mode: The stack mode is not selected. Green Steady on The stack mode is selected. The switch is a standby or slave switch in a stack, and the service port indicators show the stack ID of the switch. Green Blinking If you are not changing the indicator mode (default): The switch is the master switch in a stack or a standalone switch with the stacking function enabled. If you are changing the indicator mode: The stack mode is selected. The switch is the master switch in a stack or a standalone switch, and the service port indicators show the stack ID of the master switch. After 45 seconds, the service port indicators automatically restore to the status mode. 4 PoE PoE indicator - Off The PoE mode is not selected. Green Steady on The PoE mode is selected, and service port indicators show the PoE status of each port. 5 MODE Mode switch button - - When you press this button once, the service port indicators change to the stack mode and show the stack ID of the local switch. When you press this button a second time, the service port indicators change to the PoE mode and show the PoE status of each service port. When you press this button a third time, the service port indicators restore to the default mode and show the connection status and link activity of each service port. If you do not press the MODE button within 45 seconds, the service port indicators restore to the default mode. In this case, the PoE indicator is off. NOTE: Hold down the mode switch button for 6s and release it to start the web initial login mode. Either of the following situations will occur: If the switch has no configuration file, the system attempts to enter the web initial login mode. If the system enters the web initial login mode successfully, the MST mode indicator turns green and stays on for a maximum of 45 seconds. If the login fails, check whether the device uses factory default settings. 6 - Electrical service port indicator (one indicator for each port) Arrowheads show the positions of ports. A down arrowhead indicates a port at the bottom, and an up arrowhead indicates a port at the top. Meanings of service port indicators vary in different modes. For details, see Table 4-128 and Table 4-129 . NOTE: If a power failure occurs on a device's PCB board, indicators of the last four GE or 10GE optical ports on the device's front panel blink green cyclically at an interval of 1 second, with each indicator illuminating for 0.25 seconds. 7 - Optical service port indicator (two indicators for each port) Each optical port has two single-color indicators. The one on the left is the ACT indicator (yellow), and the one on the right is the LINK indicator (green). Arrowheads show the positions of ports. A down arrowhead indicates a port at the bottom, and an up arrowhead indicates a port at the top. 8 CLOUD Cloud indicator NOTE: In versions earlier than V600R024C00, this indicator is reserved. - Off The device is not connected to the cloud. Blue Fast blinking The device is connecting to the cloud. Blue Slow blinking The device is in the cloud management state. Table 4-128 Description of service port indicators in different modes (one indicator for each port) Display Mode Color Status Description Default mode - Off The port is not connected or has been shut down. Green Steady on A link has been established on the port. Green Blinking The port is sending or receiving data. MST stack mode - Off Port indicators do not show the stack ID of the switch. Green Steady on The switch is not the master switch in a stack. If the indicator of a port is steady on, the number of this port is the stack ID of the switch. Green <td class="idp-ltr-html-cellrowborde

products.title: Huawei eKitEngine S310S-24T4JX SWITCH

It stands out for its all-optical (SFP) downlink design, providing high-speed fiber connectivity for distributed network architectures.

Each combo port matches only one internal forwarding port. A combo port can be configured as an electrical port or an optical port, but only one port can be active at a time. When one port is active, the other port is shut down. products.title: Huawei eKitEngine S620-24T16X8Y2CZ Switch
products.subtitle: The Huawei eKitEngine S620-24T16X8Y2CZ is a flagship-tier, high-density Layer 3 managed switch designed for the core or aggregation layers of SMB and enterprise networks. It offers an exceptional port density with multi-rate speeds, ranging from standard Gigabit copper to ultra-fast 100G fiber, making it a future-proof solution for bandwidth-heavy environments.
